body:
Hi Dorothy,

Following up on the reminder schedule we set up last month for my morning routine — Topiramate, Buspirone, and Ferrous sulfate right after I get up, before the coffee's even done dripping. Mostly working, but Tuesday and Thursday mornings I've got early client intakes at Lionsgate before 8, and twice now I've rushed out the door without logging the Ferrous sulfate dose in the app even though I actually took it.

Could you push the notification window earlier on those two days — 6:15 instead of 6:45? I'm usually up by then anyway. Also wanted to check on the Buspirone refill status, I think I've got about a week left in the bottle.

Thanks for staying on top of this.

Best,
Ian

Hi Ian,

Done — adjusted the Tuesday/Thursday reminders to 6:15 AM starting this week, all three meds will ping together in that window same as your other mornings. Also added a quick "log now" button on the lock screen so you're not stuck unlocking the app if you're already halfway out the door to Lionsgate.

Good news on the Buspirone — refill request went through to your pharmacy yesterday, should be ready for pickup by Thursday. I'll send a separate alert once it's confirmed so you're not guessing.

One small suggestion: since you're already checking your mum's pill organizer most evenings before bed, you could pair that habit with logging your own morning doses — habit-stacking tends to stick better than a standalone alarm. Happy to build that in if you want it.

Take care,
Dorothy

Hi Mum,

Quick one — we're down to the last few scoops of the dark roast. Can you grab another bag when you're out, same strong blend as before, not the medium one? One cup black in the morning is really all I need before I start calendar review at the kitchen table.

Also the drip machine sputtered yesterday, took longer than usual to finish. Might just need a descale — I'll do it Saturday morning if clients don't run me over.

Thanks, see you at breakfast.

Ian

Morning Ian,

Picked up the dark roast at Giant on the way back from walking Lucy to Singer — got two bags so we're set for a few weeks. One's in the pantry, one's in the freezer like you like it.

Also ran a vinegar cycle through the drip machine this afternoon, so skip the descale Saturday. It's already brewing faster. Let me know if the taste is off, sometimes it needs a plain water rinse after.

Love, Mum

Done and done, Mum. Tried it this morning, one cup black, tastes exactly right, and the machine's back to full speed. Thank you for handling the vinegar cycle, saved me the Saturday chore.

I'll pick up more filters this weekend since we're getting low. Have a good one.

Ian

Hi Dorothy,

Following up on the sync issue I flagged Monday. Every morning I'm at the kitchen table with coffee going through email and the calendar before Mum walks Lucy to Singer Elementary, and for the third day running the desktop calendar is showing Tuesday's client intake block twice while my phone shows it once. It's making my morning review useless for planning the day at Lionsgate.

Can you check if this is a known sync bug between the web client and mobile app? I need this reliable — I'm blocking time Saturday morning for case prep and I don't want it to silently duplicate or drop.

Thanks,
Ian Woodward

Hi Ian,

Thanks for the detail — that's helpful. Duplicate all-day-adjacent blocks usually trace back to a stale device token on one client. Before I dig further, can you confirm: are you using the same Google account on both phone and desktop, and roughly what time each morning does the duplicate appear (right when you open the app, or does it show up later)?

In the meantime, try removing and re-adding the calendar account on your phone only — leave desktop untouched. That isolates whether the phone client is the one re-pushing stale data.

Best,
Dorothy

Hola Dorothy,

Did the phone re-add this morning at the kitchen table — same Google account on both, and yes, the duplicate always appears the moment I open the desktop app, not later. After removing and re-adding on phone, today's review was clean, no duplicate on the intake block. I'll know for sure after a couple more days but it looks promising.

One more thing while I have you — is there a way to get a read-only shared view so my sister Anne can see my calendar without edit access? She sometimes helps coordinate when I'm swamped with client appointments.

Thanks for the quick turnaround,
Ian

Hi Ian,

Glad the phone re-add cleared it — that confirms it was a stale token on that device, ticket's closed on our end unless it resurfaces. If it comes back, send me a screenshot of the exact timestamp and I'll pull the sync logs directly.

On sharing: yes, under Calendar Settings > Share with Specific People you can add Anne's email with "See only free/busy" or "See all event details" without giving edit rights — either works for read-only. Let me know if the permissions screen doesn't match what I described and I'll walk you through it.

Best,
Dorothy

Hi Mum,

Quick follow-up on the walk to Singer — thank you for this week, it's been a real help getting through morning calls with clients before I head into the office. I know Tuesdays are tight for you with your own errands, so if you'd rather I take Lucy those mornings, just say the word and I'll rearrange.

One thing: dismissal moves to 3:15 on Wednesdays starting next week (early release), the school sent it out yesterday. Can you confirm you got that email too? Don't want Lucy standing around waiting on the corner.

Also, Anne mentioned she might come by Saturday to see you and Lucy — no need to plan anything, I'll sort groceries myself before that.

Thanks again, Mum. Really.

Love,
Ian

Hi love,

Got the school email, yes — 3:15 Wednesdays starting next week, I've written it on the kitchen calendar so I won't forget. Honestly I don't mind the walk one bit, gives Lucy and me time to go over her spelling words, and she likes stopping to see the neighbour's dog on Fenton Street.

Tuesdays are fine, don't fuss about swapping — I'm up early regardless. If it ever gets to be too much I'll tell you plainly, you know me.

Tell Anne I'd love to see her Saturday. No need for you to do groceries either, I've already got my list ready for Giant.

Love,
Mum

Hi Dorothy,

Following up on ticket #4471 from last week — the Gmail API integration we use to pull client intake emails into our case tracker at Lionsgate Partners stopped syncing again yesterday afternoon. Same error as before: "invalid_grant, token expired or revoked." I re-ran the OAuth consent flow twice and it still drops after about an hour.

This is the intake box that logs correspondence from clients waiting on I-130 and asylum filings, so every hour it's down is a real backlog. Patricia Rivas is asking me for a status report by end of day, so I'd appreciate a quicker turnaround than last time if possible.

Can you check if something changed on the scope/consent screen config? Happy to hop on a call between 1-3pm today, I'm at the Silver Spring office all afternoon.

Thanks,
Ian Woodward

Hi Ian,

Thanks for the details — that helps narrow it down. I pulled the logs for your project and the token churn is coming from a refresh token conflict: it looks like the app's OAuth consent screen got bumped from "Testing" to needing verification when the client email scope (gmail.readonly plus the modify scope) was added last month. Refresh tokens in that state expire after 7 days, which lines up with the pattern you're seeing.

Two options: (1) I can submit for the scope verification review, which usually takes 3-5 business days, or (2) as a faster stopgap, add your own account and Patricia's as registered test users under OAuth consent screen > Test users — that gives you a 6-month token instead of 7-day expiry while verification is pending.

I'd recommend option 2 for today so your intake box stays up, and I'll file the verification request in parallel. I can walk you through the test-user step over a screen share at 1:30pm if that works — just send a calendar invite.

Best,
Dorothy
Support, Gmail API Platform Team

Dorothy,

Done — added myself and Patricia as test users under the consent screen, ran the re-auth, and the intake box has been syncing clean for the last two hours. No more invalid_grant errors. Skipped the call since the steps were exactly as you laid out.

Go ahead and file the verification request on your end so we're not back here in six months. I'll flag it on my calendar to check status in a few days.

Really appreciate the fast turnaround — made the report to Patricia a lot easier. Thanks for looking into it same-day.

Best,
Ian

Hi Dorothy,

Following up on ticket #48213 from last week about the Google Calendar API sync failing between our intake scheduling tool and my Google Workspace calendar. It's still dropping recurring events — three ESOL sessions at Montgomery College and two client intake blocks disappeared off my calendar again this morning.

Patricia needs the intake calendar reliable before Monday since we've got four new asylum case consultations booked. Can you give me a status update? Happy to hop on a call if that's faster.

Thanks,
Ian Woodward

Hi Ian,

Thanks for the follow-up, and sorry for the delay. I pulled the logs on ticket #48213 — looks like the OAuth token for your connected calendar is being refreshed but the API call is hitting a 403 insufficientPermissions error on the calendar.events.patch scope. That would explain why recurring events sync once and then vanish on the next update.

Can you confirm which Google account is connected (work or personal) and whether anyone recently changed sharing permissions on that calendar? I'd also like the exact timestamp of this morning's failure if you have it — that'll help me match it to our error logs.

I'll keep this open and prioritized given your Monday deadline.

Best,
Dorothy Woodward
Support Team

Hi Dorothy,

It's the work account — calendar.ianwoodward@lionsgatepartners.org. Nobody's changed sharing settings that I know of, though our office admin moved us to a new Workspace tier last month, so maybe permissions reset then. This morning's drop happened around 7:40 AM, right before Mum left to walk Lucy to school, so I caught it over coffee before my first client call.

I re-authorized the connection twice yesterday and it held about six hours before failing again. If it's a scope issue on your end, let me know what needs re-granting and I'll walk our IT contact through it — I'd rather not have Patricia asking me twice this week why the intake calendar's empty.

Thanks for staying on this,
Ian

Hi Ian,

Found it — the Workspace tier migration reset the OAuth scope grants, so calendar.events.patch was silently downgraded to read-only. I've pushed a fix that forces a full re-consent on next login instead of a silent token refresh, which should stop the disappearing-event pattern for good.

Please log out of the calendar integration once and log back in today — you'll see a permissions screen listing calendar.events as full access. Approve that and the sync should hold. I tested it against a dummy recurring event pattern matching your ESOL/intake mix for 24 hours with no drops.

I'm closing ticket #48213 as resolved, but reply here directly if anything slips again before Monday — I'll jump straight back on it.

Best,
Dorothy Woodward
Support Team

Hi Dorothy,

Following up on ticket #4471 from last Thursday — the Google Drive API sync for our shared case files folder is still throwing a 403 on every upload attempt. It's affecting the whole paralegal team at Lionsgate; we scan and file I-589 and I-130 supporting documents through that integration daily, and right now everything's backing up locally.

Patricia Rivas (my supervisor) is asking for a status update since we've got three client appointments Thursday morning where we need to pull documents from that folder. Is this a quota issue or did something change on the OAuth side? Happy to hop on a quick call if that's faster than email.

Thanks,
Ian Woodward
Lionsgate Partners Immigration Legal Services

Hi Ian,

Thanks for the nudge, and sorry for the delay on this one. I dug into it this morning — a security review last week rolled back the OAuth scopes on the service account tied to your Drive integration, which is what's throwing the 403s. It wasn't flagged as affecting active integrations, which it clearly did.

I've re-authorized the service account with the correct drive.file and drive.metadata scopes and pushed a fresh refresh token to your admin console. Can you have someone try an upload on your end in the next hour? It should go through clean now, but I want confirmation before I close the ticket.

If it's still erroring, call me directly at ext. 2290 — no need to wait on another email round-trip given your Thursday deadline. I'll also flag this integration so future security reviews check with your team before touching scopes.

Best,
Dorothy Woodward
IT Support

Subject: Re: WhatsApp Business API — verification still pending (Ticket #48291)

Hi Dorothy,

Following up on the ticket from last week. Our WhatsApp number for Lionsgate Partners Immigration Legal Services is still showing "pending verification" in the Business Manager, and I need it live before we start sending appointment reminders to clients. A lot of our intake folks only check WhatsApp, not email, so this is holding up actual casework.

Can you confirm what's blocking it? I submitted the Archdiocese of Washington nonprofit documentation and our Silver Spring office address like your system asked. Patricia Rivas (my supervisor) is asking me for a status update today, so a quick answer would help a lot.

Gracias,
Ian Woodward
Lionsgate Partners Immigration Legal Services

Hi Ian,

Thanks for the patience. I checked ticket #48291 — the business verification actually cleared two days ago, but the display name "Lionsgate Partners" didn't match the legal entity name on file ("Archdiocese of Washington"), so it got flagged for manual review. That's the hold-up.

Two options: submit a DBA/operating-name letter tying Lionsgate Partners to the Archdiocese, or change the display name to match exactly and request the DBA change later. Most orgs in your situation go with option one since it's faster — usually clears in 1-2 business days once the letter is uploaded.

Also, quick note: you'll want your message templates (appointment reminders, etc.) pre-approved separately, including any Spanish-language versions — those go through a second review queue. Send them whenever you're ready and I'll flag mine as priority.

Best,
Dorothy
Support Team

Dorothy,

Done — uploaded the DBA letter this morning, so that should close out the display name mismatch. I also attached two templates for approval: one English, one Spanish ("Recordatorio de su cita..."), both for appointment reminders. If those could get flagged priority like you mentioned, I'd appreciate it — we've got a full Saturday morning block of client appointments starting in a couple weeks and I'd like the reminders live before then.

Emailed Patricia the update so she's not waiting on me. If anything else is needed from our end just let me know — I'm at the Silver Spring office most mornings and check this inbox daily.

Thanks again for walking me through the fix.

Best,
Ian

Hi Dorothy,

Thanks for the note. Tomorrow afternoon works — does 3pm fit?

Best,
Ian

3pm is great. Sending the calendar invite now.

Dorothy

Got it, see you then.

Ian

Quick reminder for our chat in 30 min.

Dorothy

Hi Dorothy,

Following up on my note from last week about the drip coffee subscription — I asked to switch the order to the stronger roast, one bag at a time, since I'm only brewing a single strong cup each morning before I head into the office. Wanted to check whether that change actually went through, because my last shipment still came as the regular blend.

Mornings are tight for me — I'm usually out the door for client appointments at Lionsgate Partners not long after that first cup, so I'd rather not have to double-brew to get it strong enough. If it's easier, I'm fine paying a bit more for the dark roast option instead of adjusting the regular blend.

Can you confirm the next shipment date and which roast is actually queued up? Appreciate you sorting this out.

Best,
Ian Woodward

Hi Ian,

Thanks for following up, and sorry about the mix-up — I checked your account and you're right, the switch to the dark roast didn't save properly on our end the first time. I've corrected it now: your subscription is set to the strong dark roast, one bag per shipment, and I've applied a small credit to your account for the inconvenience.

Your next shipment is scheduled to go out this Thursday, and everything after that will come as the dark roast automatically — no need to call in again unless something changes. Given you're brewing it strong for a single cup, I'd also recommend the slightly coarser grind option if you're using a drip machine; it tends to hold up better without turning bitter.

Let me know if Thursday's shipment doesn't reflect the change once it ships, and I'll jump right on it.

Best,
Dorothy Woodward
Customer Support

Hi Mum,

Wanted to follow up on our kitchen table routine now that summer session's started at Montgomery College. I think reviewing the calendar together over coffee each morning is actually working well — it's helping me catch Lucy's early pickup days and my court dates in the same glance instead of scrambling at 8am.

Could we add one thing though? On days I have a hearing downtown, could you flag it on the whiteboard the night before so it's the first thing I see when I come down for coffee? I'll keep doing the same for your appointments so neither of us is caught off guard.

Also — thank you for walking Lucy to Singer again this week. It's been a big help with Patricia loading extra case reviews onto my plate at Lionsgate.

Love,
Ian

Morning love,

Happy to keep it up — I actually like our little coffee routine, gives me a chance to see your day before the house gets busy. I'll write hearing days on the whiteboard in red so they stand out, and I'll do the same for my doctor visits so you're not blindsided either.

Lucy was singing the whole walk to Singer yesterday, something about a class pet hamster. She's already asking if Michael is visiting again soon — might be nice to pencil that in on the calendar too if he's free before your fall term starts.

I'll double check my pill organizer tonight and top it up for the week while I'm at it, so that's one less thing on your list.

Love you,
Mum

Hi Mum,

Perfect, that works. Whiteboard's already got Thursday's hearing on it in red — easy to spot with my first cup. Thank you.

I texted Michael and he's tentatively free the weekend after next, so I penciled him in on the kitchen calendar for Saturday morning — figured Lucy would want to show him her hamster story firsthand. I'll confirm with him by midweek.

Also squared things away with Patricia — she's shifting my Friday intake appointments earlier so I can still be home for Lucy's pickup those days. Small win.

Talk soon,
Ian

Hi Mum,

Quick follow-up on the Singer Elementary run — thank you for taking that over this week, it's been a huge help with my morning appointments backed up. How's Lucy doing on the walk? She was worried about the crossing guard changing shifts at Fenton, wanted to check if that's still sorted.

Also, heads up: Thursday I've got a client meeting that could run long, so if you're doing pickup that day too, might be closer to 3:15 than 3:00. I'll confirm by Wednesday night.

Thanks again — really does make the mornings easier.

Ian

Hi Ian,

All going well. Lucy chats the whole way, mostly about her class hamster. The crossing guard's still there, same as always, so no worries there.

Thursday's fine, I can wait a bit longer if your meeting runs over — just text me if it's going to be much past 3:30 since I'll want to start dinner. One thing: the crosswalk by the church on Colesville gets busy around drop-off time, so we've started cutting through the side street instead. Adds two minutes but feels safer.

Let me know about Wednesday when you know more.

Love, Mum

Hi Mum,

That side street route sounds smart, glad you two worked that out. Thursday's confirmed — meeting should wrap by 3:00 but I'll aim to be back by 3:30 at the latest, will text if anything changes.

Lucy mentioned the hamster to me too, sounds like it's the whole personality of her class this year. If it rains this week can you make sure she's got the yellow raincoat, not the denim jacket — she'll fight you on it but the jacket's not waterproof.

Thanks for all of this, seriously. Between Lionsgate and evening ESOL prep I wouldn't be able to manage the school run most days.

Ian

Hi Ian,

Will do on the raincoat — already fought that battle once this week and won. Thursday's set, I'll text you if it looks like it'll be past 3:30 either way so we're not both guessing.

Honestly don't mention it, I like the walk and the time with her. Her teacher mentioned at pickup that she's been more chatty in class lately too, so maybe the mornings are helping settle her in.

Go easy on yourself this week — you've got a lot on.

Love, Mum
